@@ -1,3 +1,16 @@
+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-0oi","title":"Task: Expand extract-functions.mjs to export all testable functions","description":"## What
Expand extract-functions.mjs to extract and export all testable pure functions from the embedded JavaScript.

## Current Exports
- ExitCodes
- detectPlatform
- validateContentType
- validateMagicBytes
- validateImageContent

## New Exports Needed
- formatJsonSuccess
- formatJsonError
- extractGooglePhotosBaseUrl
- getExifDateTime (requires exifr - may need async handling)
- generateFilename
- parseGoogleDriveId
- getGoogleDriveDownloadUrl
- getGoogleDrivePreviewUrl
- ErrorMap

## Implementation

Add new extractors:
```javascript
const extractors = [
  // ... existing ...
  
  // JSON formatting
  { name: 'formatJsonSuccess', pattern: /function formatJsonSuccess\(data\) \{[\s\S]*?^\}/m },
  { name: 'formatJsonError', pattern: /function formatJsonError\(code, message, remediation.*?\) \{[\s\S]*?^\}/m },
  
  // Google Photos
  { name: 'extractGooglePhotosBaseUrl', pattern: /function extractGooglePhotosBaseUrl\(cdnUrl\) \{[\s\S]*?^\}/m },
  
  // Google Drive
  { name: 'parseGoogleDriveId', pattern: /function parseGoogleDriveId\(url\) \{[\s\S]*?^\}/m },
  { name: 'getGoogleDriveDownloadUrl', pattern: /function getGoogleDriveDownloadUrl\(fileId\) \{[\s\S]*?^\}/m },
  { name: 'getGoogleDrivePreviewUrl', pattern: /function getGoogleDrivePreviewUrl\(fileId\) \{[\s\S]*?^\}/m },
  
  // Error mapping
  { name: 'ErrorMap', pattern: /const ErrorMap = \{[\s\S]*?\};/ },
];
```

## Special Cases

### getExifDateTime
This function uses the 'exifr' library which may not be available in test context.
Options:
1. Mock exifr for unit tests
2. Make test depend on exifr being installed
3. Create a wrapper that's easier to test

### generateFilename
May have dependencies on Date - ensure tests mock date for determinism.

## Validation
After updating, run:
```bash
node scripts/tests/extract-functions.mjs > /tmp/test.mjs
node --check /tmp/test.mjs  # Verify syntax
grep 'export {' /tmp/test.mjs  # Verify exports
```

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-3ry","title":"Task: Create test fixtures directory with sample images","description":"## What
Create test fixtures directory with real image files for unit tests.

## Directory Structure
```
scripts/tests/fixtures/
├── images/
│   ├── sample-jpeg-with-exif.jpg   # JPEG with known EXIF datetime
│   ├── sample-jpeg-no-exif.jpg     # JPEG without EXIF
│   ├── sample-png.png              # Basic PNG
│   ├── sample-webp.webp            # WebP format
│   └── sample-heic.heic            # HEIC (if available)
├── magic-bytes/
│   ├── jpeg.bin                    # FF D8 FF E0 00 10 4A 46 49 46
│   ├── png.bin                     # 89 50 4E 47 0D 0A 1A 0A
│   ├── html.bin                    # 3C 21 44 4F 43 54 59 50 45
│   └── heic.bin                    # 00 00 00 18 66 74 79 70 68 65 69 63
└── expected-hashes/
    └── README.md                   # Explains hash storage
```

## Sample JPEG with EXIF Requirements
- Known DateTimeOriginal: 2024-01-15T10:30:00
- Small file size (<100KB for fast tests)
- Royalty-free source

## Creation Script
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/tests/create-fixtures.sh

# Generate minimal JPEG with EXIF
# Using ImageMagick + exiftool

convert -size 100x100 xc:blue /tmp/sample.jpg
exiftool -DateTimeOriginal='2024:01:15 10:30:00' /tmp/sample.jpg
mv /tmp/sample.jpg scripts/tests/fixtures/images/sample-jpeg-with-exif.jpg

# Generate magic byte files
printf '\xFF\xD8\xFF\xE0\x00\x10\x4A\x46\x49\x46' > scripts/tests/fixtures/magic-bytes/jpeg.bin
printf '\x89\x50\x4E\x47\x0D\x0A\x1A\x0A' > scripts/tests/fixtures/magic-bytes/png.bin
printf '<!DOCTYPE html>' > scripts/tests/fixtures/magic-bytes/html.bin
```

## Notes
- Commit fixtures to git (small files only)
- Document source/license for images
- Include creation script for reproducibility

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-4gr","title":"Task: Enhance CI pipeline with E2E test matrix","description":"## What
Enhance .github/workflows/ci.yml with E2E test matrix for all platforms.

## Current CI Jobs
- shellcheck
- syntax-check
- unit-tests
- install-test (ubuntu/macos matrix)
- real-link-test (iCloud only, schedule/manual)
- version-consistency

## New CI Jobs

### e2e-platform-tests
```yaml
e2e-platform-tests:
  name: E2E Platform Tests
  needs: [shellcheck, syntax-check, unit-tests]
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  timeout-minutes: 30
  # Only run on schedule or manual trigger (avoid burning API credits)
  if: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' || github.event_name == 'schedule' }}
  strategy:
    fail-fast: false
    matrix:
      platform:
        - icloud
        - dropbox
        - google-photos
        # google-drive excluded (experimental, requires manual testing)
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Setup Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      with:
        node-version: '20'
    - name: Cache dependencies
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: .ci-cache
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-giil-${{ hashFiles('giil') }}
    - name: Run ${{ matrix.platform }} test
      run: bash scripts/tests/e2e/${{ matrix.platform }}.test.sh
      env:
        GIIL_HOME: ${{ github.workspace }}/.ci-cache/giil
```

### e2e-output-modes
```yaml
e2e-output-modes:
  name: E2E Output Mode Tests
  needs: [e2e-platform-tests]
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Test output modes
      run: bash scripts/tests/e2e/output-modes.test.sh
```

## Artifact Upload on Failure
Add step to upload debug artifacts if tests fail:
```yaml
    - name: Upload debug artifacts
      if: failure()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: debug-${{ matrix.platform }}
        path: |
          .ci-output/
          giil_debug_*.png
          giil_debug_*.html
```

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-4z2","title":"Task: Migrate existing real_link_test.sh to new E2E structure","description":"## What
Migrate the existing scripts/real_link_test.sh to the new E2E test structure with enhanced logging.

## Current Location
scripts/real_link_test.sh

## New Location
scripts/tests/e2e/icloud.test.sh

## Changes Required

### 1. Add logging library
```bash
source "$(dirname "$0")/../lib/log.sh"
```

### 2. Replace echo with log_* functions
```bash
# Before:
echo '[error] Output file missing'

# After:
log_fail 'Output file missing'
```

### 3. Add timing metrics
```bash
start_time=$(date +%s.%N)
# ... run giil ...
end_time=$(date +%s.%N)
duration=$(echo "$end_time - $start_time" | bc)
log_info "Duration: ${duration}s"
```

### 4. Add JSON schema validation
```bash
# Verify v3 schema fields
echo "$output" | jq -e '.ok and .schema_version and .platform' || log_fail 'Missing v3 schema fields'
```

### 5. Keep backward compatibility
Create symlink from old location:
```bash
ln -sf tests/e2e/icloud.test.sh scripts/real_link_test.sh
```

## Testing
After migration:
```bash
bash scripts/tests/e2e/icloud.test.sh
# Should produce structured output with PASS/FAIL
```

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-54j","title":"Task: Unit tests for Google Drive ID parsing (parseGoogleDriveId)","description":"## What
Test parseGoogleDriveId() and related URL builder functions.

## Functions to Test
1. parseGoogleDriveId(url) - Extracts file ID from various Drive URL formats
2. getGoogleDriveDownloadUrl(fileId) - Creates /uc?export=download URL
3. getGoogleDrivePreviewUrl(fileId) - Creates /file/d/.../view URL

## Test Cases for parseGoogleDriveId
1. drive.google.com/file/d/XXX/view
2. drive.google.com/open?id=XXX
3. docs.google.com/file/d/XXX/view
4. docs.google.com/document/d/XXX (Google Doc, not image)
5. URL with extra query params
6. Invalid/malformed URL
7. Non-Drive URL

## Test Cases for URL builders
1. Valid file ID produces correct URL structure
2. Empty file ID handled gracefully

## File
scripts/tests/google-drive.test.mjs

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-84w","title":"Task: Unit tests for EXIF datetime extraction (getExifDateTime)","description":"## What
Test getExifDateTime() function that extracts datetime from image EXIF metadata.

## Function Behavior
Returns ISO 8601 datetime string or null if not found.

## Test Cases
1. JPEG with DateTimeOriginal field
2. JPEG with only DateTimeDigitized (fallback)
3. JPEG with only DateTime (fallback)
4. JPEG with no datetime fields
5. PNG (no EXIF support typically)
6. HEIC with datetime
7. Corrupt/truncated buffer
8. Empty buffer

## Test Fixtures Required
- scripts/tests/fixtures/images/sample-jpeg-with-exif.jpg
- Create with known datetime for deterministic test

## File
scripts/tests/exif-datetime.test.mjs

## Note
Uses 'exifr' library internally - tests should use real image buffers

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-8x5","title":"Task: E2E test for Google Drive platform (experimental)","description":"## What
Create E2E integration test for Google Drive downloads. Marked as [experimental].

## Test Script
scripts/tests/e2e/google-drive.test.sh

## Prerequisites
- Need a stable Google Drive test URL (shared as 'Anyone with link')
- Store expected SHA256 in fixtures

## Test Steps
1. Download from drive.google.com/file/d/.../view URL
2. Verify file downloaded (may be lower resolution)
3. Verify JSON output schema (platform: 'gdrive')
4. Log any auth detection

## Verbose Logging
```
[TIME] [INFO] Platform: gdrive [EXPERIMENTAL]
[TIME] [INFO] URL: https://drive.google.com/file/d/...
[TIME] [INFO] Attempting direct download...
[TIME] [WARN] Direct download failed, trying thumbnail extraction
[TIME] [INFO] Downloaded via thumbnail: 1920x1080 (thumbnail quality)
[TIME] [WARN] Full resolution not available (expected for Drive)
[TIME] [PASS] Downloaded successfully with fallback
```

## Error Scenario Test
Also test with a NON-public file to verify auth detection:
```
[TIME] [INFO] Testing auth detection...
[TIME] [INFO] Using restricted test URL
[TIME] [FAIL] Exit code 11 (AUTH_REQUIRED) - expected
[TIME] [PASS] Auth detection working correctly
```

## Notes
- Google Drive is experimental - quality may vary
- Auth detection is critical to avoid capturing login pages
- Test should pass even if resolution is degraded

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-9ei","title":"FEATURE: Test Infrastructure - Logging, Fixtures, CI Pipeline","description":"## Overview

Build robust test infrastructure that supports both unit and E2E tests with:
- Verbose structured logging for CI debugging
- Real test fixtures (actual image files, not mocks)
- CI pipeline enhancements
- Local development convenience scripts

## Logging Framework

### Requirements
- JSON-structured logs for CI parsing
- Human-readable format for local dev
- Timing metrics for performance regression detection
- Clear PASS/FAIL status per test

### Implementation
```bash
# scripts/tests/lib/log.sh
log_info() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [INFO] $*" >&2; }
log_pass() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [PASS] $*" >&2; }
log_fail() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [FAIL] $*" >&2; }
log_json() { echo "{\"ts\":\"$(date -Iseconds)\",\"level\":\"$1\",\"msg\":\"$2\"}" >&2; }
```

## Test Fixtures

### scripts/tests/fixtures/
```
fixtures/
├── images/
│   ├── sample-jpeg-with-exif.jpg    # Real JPEG with EXIF datetime
│   ├── sample-png.png               # Basic PNG
│   ├── sample-heic.heic             # HEIC for conversion tests
│   └── sample-webp.webp             # WebP format
├── magic-bytes/
│   ├── jpeg-magic.bin               # First 12 bytes of JPEG
│   ├── html-magic.bin               # HTML document start
│   └── heic-magic.bin               # HEIC ftyp header
└── expected-hashes/
    ├── icloud.sha256
    ├── dropbox.sha256
    └── google-photos.sha256
```

### Source for Fixtures
- Use royalty-free test images
- Generate minimal valid images programmatically where possible
- Store actual EXIF data for datetime extraction tests

## CI Pipeline Enhancements

### .github/workflows/ci.yml additions

```yaml
# Add after existing unit-tests job
e2e-tests:
  name: E2E Integration Tests
  needs: [shellcheck, syntax-check, unit-tests]
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  timeout-minutes: 30
  strategy:
    fail-fast: false
    matrix:
      platform: [icloud, dropbox, google-photos]
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Setup dependencies
      run: ...
    - name: Run ${{ matrix.platform }} test
      run: bash scripts/tests/e2e/${{ matrix.platform }}.test.sh
      env:
        GIIL_LOG_FORMAT: json
```

## Local Development Scripts

### scripts/tests/run-all.sh
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run all tests: unit + e2e

echo '=== Unit Tests ==='
./scripts/tests/run-tests.sh

echo '=== E2E Tests ==='
for test in ./scripts/tests/e2e/*.test.sh; do
  echo "--- $(basename $test) ---"
  bash "$test"
done
```

### scripts/tests/run-quick.sh
```bash
# Fast tests only (unit tests, skip live E2E)
node --test scripts/tests/*.test.mjs
```

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-aqm","title":"Task: E2E tests for album mode (--all)","description":"## What
Create E2E tests for album mode that downloads all photos from a shared album.

## Test Script
scripts/tests/e2e/album-mode.test.sh

## Prerequisites
- Need test albums with known photo counts:
  - iCloud album with 3 photos
  - Google Photos album with 3 photos

## Test Steps
```bash
# Create temp output dir
output_dir=$(mktemp -d)

# Download album
giil 'ALBUM_URL' --all --output "$output_dir" --json > output.json

# Verify correct number of files
count=$(ls "$output_dir"/*.jpg | wc -l)
assert_equal "$count" 3

# Verify each file is valid image
for f in "$output_dir"/*.jpg; do
  file "$f" | grep -q 'JPEG' || fail "Invalid image: $f"
done
```

## Verbose Logging
```
[TIME] [INFO] Album mode enabled
[TIME] [INFO] URL: https://share.icloud.com/photos/...
[TIME] [INFO] Detecting album structure...
[TIME] [INFO] Found 3 photos in album
[TIME] [INFO] Downloading 1/3...
[TIME] [INFO] Downloading 2/3...
[TIME] [INFO] Downloading 3/3...
[TIME] [PASS] All 3 photos downloaded successfully
```

## Edge Cases
- Empty album (should exit gracefully)
- Single-photo 'album' (should work like normal mode)
- Very large album (test with limit if >10 photos)

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-ci6","title":"Task: E2E tests for output modes (--json, --base64, --preserve, --convert)","description":"## What
Create E2E tests that verify all output mode flags work correctly.

## Test Script
scripts/tests/e2e/output-modes.test.sh

## Output Modes to Test

### --json
```bash
output=$(giil 'URL' --json)
# Verify valid JSON
echo "$output" | jq .
# Verify required fields
echo "$output" | jq -e '.ok and .schema_version and .platform and .path'
```

### --base64
```bash
output=$(giil 'URL' --base64)
# Verify valid base64 (decode should work)
echo "$output" | base64 -d > /tmp/test.jpg
file /tmp/test.jpg | grep -q 'JPEG'
```

### --base64 --json (combined)
```bash
output=$(giil 'URL' --base64 --json)
# Verify JSON with base64 field instead of path
echo "$output" | jq -e '.base64'
```

### --preserve
```bash
# Download with --preserve (skip MozJPEG)
giil 'URL' --preserve --json
# Verify file size matches original (larger than default compressed)
```

### --convert webp
```bash
giil 'URL' --convert webp --json
# Verify output extension is .webp
output=$(giil 'URL' --convert webp --json)
echo "$output" | jq -r '.path' | grep -q '\.webp$'
```

### --quality 50
```bash
# Download with lower quality
giil 'URL' --quality 50 --json
# Verify smaller file size
```

## Verbose Logging
Log file sizes to verify compression is working:
```
[TIME] [INFO] Testing output modes...
[TIME] [INFO] Default (MozJPEG): 456 KB
[TIME] [INFO] --preserve: 1.2 MB
[TIME] [INFO] --quality 50: 234 KB
[TIME] [INFO] --convert webp: 189 KB
[TIME] [PASS] All output modes working
```

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-emm","title":"Task: Unit tests for Google Photos URL extraction (extractGooglePhotosBaseUrl)","description":"## What
Test extractGooglePhotosBaseUrl() function that transforms CDN URLs for full-resolution access.

## Function Behavior
```javascript
// Input: https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/xxx=w1920-h1080
// Output: { baseUrl: 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/xxx', fullResUrl: 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/xxx=s0' }
```

## Test Cases
1. Standard CDN URL with size modifier (=w1920-h1080)
2. CDN URL with =s suffix (=s1920)
3. CDN URL without any modifier
4. Non-Google Photos URL (should return unchanged)
5. Empty string / null input
6. URL with query parameters after modifier

## File
scripts/tests/google-photos.test.mjs

## Notes
- The =s0 modifier requests maximum resolution
- This is the key optimization for Google Photos full-res downloads

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-es6","title":"Task: E2E test for Dropbox platform","description":"## What
Create E2E integration test for Dropbox downloads using the fast path (no Playwright).

## Test Script
scripts/tests/e2e/dropbox.test.sh

## Prerequisites
- Need a stable Dropbox test URL (user to provide/create)
- Store expected SHA256 in fixtures

## Test Steps
1. Download from Dropbox /s/ format URL
2. Verify no Playwright was used (fast path)
3. Verify output file exists and is valid image
4. Verify JSON output schema (platform: 'dropbox', method: 'direct')
5. Log timing (should be 1-3 seconds, not 5-15)
6. Verify SHA256 matches expected

## Verbose Logging
```
[TIME] [INFO] Platform: dropbox
[TIME] [INFO] Testing fast path (no browser)
[TIME] [INFO] URL: https://www.dropbox.com/s/...
[TIME] [INFO] Download started
[TIME] [INFO] Download complete: 2.4 MB in 1.8s
[TIME] [INFO] Method: direct (expected)
[TIME] [PASS] SHA256 verified
[TIME] [PASS] Total time: 2.1s (under 5s threshold)
```

## Also Test
- /scl/fi/ URL format (alternate sharing format)
- Raw URL with existing dl=0 parameter
- Raw URL with existing raw=1 parameter (should work)

## Exit Codes to Verify
- Exit 0: Success
- Exit 10: Network timeout (if Dropbox is down)
- Exit 11: Auth required (password-protected link)
- Exit 12: Not found (deleted/expired)

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm","title":"EPIC: giil v3.0 Multi-Platform Expansion","description":"## Overview

Transform giil from an iCloud-only tool into a universal cloud image downloader supporting Dropbox, Google Photos, and Google Drive (experimental).

## Strategic Context

giil ("Get Image [from] Internet Link", originally "Get iCloud Image Link") serves a critical workflow: enabling AI coding assistants to view images shared from mobile devices during remote SSH sessions. Expanding platform support multiplies the tool's utility without changing its core value proposition.

## Key Insights from Research (Jan 2026)

1. **Dropbox is trivial**: Simple URL parameter change (dl=0 → raw=1) enables direct curl downloads. No browser needed. 1-3s vs 5-15s.

2. **Google Photos is solvable**: CDN URLs have size modifiers. The =s0 suffix returns original resolution. Browser needed only for URL extraction, not download.

3. **Google Drive is conditional**: 2024 API changes broke direct URLs. Requires "Anyone with the link" sharing. Ship as experimental.

## Implementation Phases

1. **Core Infrastructure** - Platform detection, adapter interface, content validation
2. **Dropbox Support** - Direct URL downloads, no Playwright needed
3. **Google Photos Support** - CDN URL extraction + =s0 modifier
4. **Google Drive (Experimental)** - Multi-tier with auth detection
5. **Output & Error Handling** - New exit codes, JSON schema
6. **Testing Infrastructure** - Unit, replay, and live tests
7. **Documentation & Release** - README rewrite, v3.0.0 release

## Success Criteria

- All existing iCloud commands work unchanged (backward compatibility)
- Dropbox links download at full resolution without Playwright
- Google Photos links download at full resolution with =s0 modifier
- Google Drive links work for properly shared files (with clear errors for restricted files)
- New JSON schema with schema_version, ok, platform fields
- Extended exit codes for better scripting (0-20)
- Comprehensive documentation for all platforms

## Non-Goals (Critical Boundaries)

- No bypassing authentication or CAPTCHAs
- No credential storage or harvesting
- No "bulk scraping" positioning
- No silent quality degradation

## Version Target: v3.0.0

Major version bump due to:
- New default behavior (preserve original bytes vs MozJPEG compression)
- JSON schema additions (breaking for strict parsers)
- Extended exit codes

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.1","title":"EPIC: Phase 1 - Core Infrastructure","description":"## Purpose

Build the foundational components that all platform adapters will depend on. This phase establishes patterns and utilities that ensure consistency across platforms.

## Why This Matters

Without a solid foundation, each platform implementation becomes a one-off with duplicated logic. The Platform Adapter interface ensures:
- Consistent URL detection and normalization
- Shared content validation (prevent HTML-as-image bugs)
- Unified error handling and exit codes
- Testable, modular architecture

## Key Components

1. **Platform Detection** - Regex-based URL pattern matching in both bash and JS
2. **Content Validation** - MIME type checking, magic bytes verification
3. **Exit Code Framework** - Extended codes 10-13, 20 for scripting
4. **Adapter Interface** - TypeScript/JSDoc interface definition

## Design Decision: Dual Implementation

Platform detection must exist in BOTH bash and JavaScript layers:
- **Bash**: Decides whether to skip Playwright entirely (Dropbox fast path)
- **JavaScript**: Used within extractor for platform-specific capture strategies

The implementations must stay synchronized. Consider extracting to a shared format (JSON config) that both layers read.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] detect_platform() works in bash for all 4 platforms
- [ ] detectPlatform() works in JS for all 4 platforms
- [ ] Content-Type validation rejects HTML with image extensions
- [ ] Magic bytes validation detects JPEG/PNG/HEIC/WebP
- [ ] All new exit codes are documented and used consistently

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.1.1","title":"Implement detect_platform() in bash","description":"## What

Add a detect_platform() function to the giil bash script that identifies which cloud service a URL belongs to.

## Implementation

```bash
detect_platform() {
    local url="$1"
    case "$url" in
        *share.icloud.com/photos/* | *icloud.com/photos/*)
            echo "icloud" ;;
        *dropbox.com/s/* | *dropbox.com/scl/fi/* | *dropbox.com/sh/*)
            echo "dropbox" ;;
        *photos.app.goo.gl/* | *photos.google.com/share/*)
            echo "gphotos" ;;
        *drive.google.com/file/d/* | *drive.google.com/open?id=*)
            echo "gdrive" ;;
        *)
            echo "unknown" ;;
    esac
}
```

## Why Case Patterns

Case statements are faster than regex in bash and more readable. The patterns cover:
- **iCloud**: Both share.icloud.com and www.icloud.com formats
- **Dropbox**: /s/ (legacy), /scl/fi/ (new), /sh/ (folders)
- **Google Photos**: Short links (goo.gl) and full links
- **Google Drive**: /file/d/ and /open?id= formats

## Testing

```bash
# Should output: icloud
detect_platform "https://share.icloud.com/photos/abc123"

# Should output: dropbox
detect_platform "https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/xyz/photo.png?dl=0"

# Should output: gphotos
detect_platform "https://photos.app.goo.gl/abc123"

# Should output: gdrive
detect_platform "https://drive.google.com/file/d/xyz/view"

# Should output: unknown
detect_platform "https://example.com/photo.jpg"
```

## Location in Script

Add near the URL normalization functions, around line 1400 in giil.

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.7.1","title":"Rewrite README for multi-platform support","description":"## What

Comprehensively update README.md to reflect multi-platform support, rebrand messaging, and v3 changes.

## Header Rebrand

FROM:
```markdown
# Get iCloud Image Link
Download full-resolution images from iCloud photo shares
```

TO:
```markdown
# giil
### Get Image [from] Internet Link

Download full-resolution images from cloud sharing links.
Originally built for iCloud, now supporting Dropbox, Google Photos, and Google Drive.
```

## New Platform Support Table

```markdown
## Supported Platforms

| Platform | Method | Speed | Full Resolution | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|-----------------|-------|
| iCloud | Playwright (4-tier) | 5-15s | ✅ Always | Original and best-supported |
| Dropbox | Direct URL | 1-3s | ✅ Always | No browser needed! |
| Google Photos | URL extraction | 5-10s | ✅ Always | Uses =s0 modifier |
| Google Drive | Playwright | 5-15s | Conditional | [Experimental] Requires public sharing |
```

## Per-Platform Examples

```markdown
## Quick Examples

### iCloud
\`\`\`bash
giil "https://share.icloud.com/photos/abc123"
\`\`\`

### Dropbox (fastest!)
\`\`\`bash
giil "https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/xxx/photo.png?dl=0"
# Direct download, no browser needed
\`\`\`

### Google Photos
\`\`\`bash
giil "https://photos.app.goo.gl/abc123"
# Also works with album links for --all mode
\`\`\`

### Google Drive [experimental]
\`\`\`bash
giil "https://drive.google.com/file/d/xxx/view"
# File must be shared as "Anyone with the link"
\`\`\`
```

## Per-Platform Troubleshooting

```markdown
## Troubleshooting

### Dropbox
- **"Received HTML instead of image"**: Link may be password-protected or expired
- **Empty output**: Verify the file is publicly shared

### Google Photos  
- **"AUTH_REQUIRED"**: Album is private. Ask owner to make it public.
- **Low resolution**: Ensure giil is using =s0 modifier (check with --verbose)

### Google Drive
- **"File is not publicly shared"**: Owner must set "Anyone with the link" access
- **Quality warning**: Drive may return lower-resolution thumbnails as fallback
```

## v3 Changes Section

```markdown
## Upgrading from v2.x

### Breaking Changes
- **Exit codes 4 and 5 changed**: Network errors now exit 10, auth errors exit 11
- **JSON schema additions**: New fields `schema_version`, `ok`, `platform`

### Behavior Changes
- **Original bytes preserved by default**: Use `--optimize` for MozJPEG compression
- **New flags**: `--print-url`, `--verbose`, `--trace`, `--convert`, `--optimize`

### Migration
\`\`\`bash
# If you were checking exit codes:
# v2: if [ $? -eq 4 ]; then  →  v3: if [ $? -eq 10 ]; then
# v2: if [ $? -eq 5 ]; then  →  v3: if [ $? -eq 11 ]; then

# If you relied on automatic JPEG compression:
# v2: giil "https://..."
# v3: giil "https://..." --optimize
\`\`\`
```

## File Size Considerations

Current README is comprehensive. Be careful not to bloat it. Consider:
- Keep main README focused on quick-start
- Add PLATFORMS.md for deep platform-specific docs (if needed)
- Link to GitHub wiki for edge cases

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.7.2","title":"Update AGENTS.md architecture diagram","description":"## What

Update the architecture diagram in AGENTS.md to reflect v3 changes, specifically the Dropbox fast path that bypasses extractor.mjs.

## Current Diagram (INCORRECT for v3)

```
        ┌─────────┐          ┌──────────┐          ┌──────────┐
        │ Dropbox │          │  Google  │          │  iCloud/ │
        │ (curl)  │          │  Photos  │          │  GDrive  │
        └─────────┘          └──────────┘          └──────────┘
             │                     │                     │
             ▼                     ▼                     ▼
        ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
        │              extractor.mjs (Playwright)                  │
```

This shows Dropbox going to extractor.mjs, but v3 bypasses it entirely!

## Corrected Diagram (v3)

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    URL Input                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                              ▼
                    ┌──────────────────┐
                    │ detect_platform()│
                    └──────────────────┘
                              │
        ┌─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┐
        ▼                     ▼                     ▼
   ┌─────────┐          ┌──────────┐          ┌──────────┐
   │ Dropbox │          │  Google  │          │  iCloud/ │
   │ FAST    │          │  Photos  │          │  GDrive  │
   └─────────┘          └──────────┘          └──────────┘
        │                     │                     │
        │                     ▼                     ▼
        │            ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
        │            │        extractor.mjs (Playwright)        │
        │            │                                           │
        │            │   TIER 1: Download button (9 selectors)   │
        │            │      ↓ fail                               │
        │            │   TIER 2: Network CDN interception        │
        │            │      ↓ fail                               │
        │            │   TIER 3: Element screenshot              │
        │            │      ↓ fail                               │
        │            │   TIER 4: Viewport screenshot             │
        │            └─────────────────────────────────────────┘
        │                           │
        ▼                           ▼
   ┌─────────┐            ┌──────────────────┐
   │  curl   │            │ Image Processing │
   │ raw=1   │            │  Sharp + MozJPEG │
   └─────────┘            │  EXIF extraction │
        │                 │  HEIC conversion │
        │                 └──────────────────┘
        │                           │
        └───────────────┬───────────┘
                        ▼
              ┌──────────────────┐
              │     Output       │
              │ (file/json/b64)  │
              └──────────────────┘
```

## Key Changes to Highlight

1. **Dropbox exits early** - Direct curl download, no Playwright
2. **Platform-specific paths** - Each platform has tailored strategy
3. **Shared output layer** - All paths converge for final output

## Also Update

1. Exit codes table (add new codes 10-13, 20)
2. CLI options table (add new flags)
3. Supported Platforms table (already present, verify accuracy)

- 1 integration test (iCloud only, no Dropbox/Google Photos/Google Drive)
- No error scenario tests

## Goal

Achieve comprehensive test coverage using REAL tests (no mocks/fakes) with detailed logging for debugging. All tests should:
1. Test actual functionality against real or realistic data
2. Provide verbose, structured logging for CI debugging
3. Cover all code paths and edge cases
4. Support both local development and CI execution

## Major Gaps to Fill

- URL normalization (normalize_url, normalize_dropbox_url)
- EXIF datetime extraction (getExifDateTime)
- Filename generation (generateFilename)
- JSON output formatting (formatJsonSuccess, formatJsonError)
- Google Photos URL extraction (extractGooglePhotosBaseUrl)

### Integration Tests (E2E)
- Multi-platform: iCloud, Dropbox, Google Photos, Google Drive
- Error scenarios: timeout, auth required, not found, unsupported type
- Output modes: --json, --base64, --preserve, --convert
- Album mode: --all for different platforms
- CLI flags: --verbose, --trace, --print-url, --debug-dir

### Bash Layer Tests
- CLI argument parsing
- Platform detection (bash version)
- Dependency detection
- Version consistency

### Infrastructure
- Verbose structured logging (JSON format for CI parsing)
- Test fixtures with real image files
- Replay tests for deterministic results

## Success Criteria
- 90%+ code coverage on testable pure functions
- All 4 platforms have at least 1 live integration test
- All exit codes have corresponding error scenario tests
- All CLI flags have at least 1 test
- Detailed logging makes CI failures debuggable

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-i8w","title":"Task: Create test logging framework (lib/log.sh)","description":"## What
Create a shared logging library for all E2E test scripts with:
- Timestamped log messages
- Color-coded severity levels
- JSON output mode for CI parsing
- Pass/fail counters

## File
scripts/tests/lib/log.sh

## Implementation
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Test logging library

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Counters
PASS_COUNT=0
FAIL_COUNT=0

timestamp() { date -Iseconds; }

log_info() { echo -e "${BLUE}[$(timestamp)] [INFO]${NC} $*" >&2; }
log_warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[$(timestamp)] [WARN]${NC} $*" >&2; }
log_pass() { 
  echo -e "${GREEN}[$(timestamp)] [PASS]${NC} $*" >&2
  ((PASS_COUNT++))
}
log_fail() {
  echo -e "${RED}[$(timestamp)] [FAIL]${NC} $*" >&2
  ((FAIL_COUNT++))
}

# JSON mode (for CI)
log_json() {
  local level=$1 msg=$2
  echo "{\"ts\":\"$(timestamp)\",\"level\":\"$level\",\"msg\":\"$msg\"}"
}

# Summary at end of test
test_summary() {
  echo ""
  echo "=== Test Summary ==="
  echo "Passed: $PASS_COUNT"
  echo "Failed: $FAIL_COUNT"
  if [[ $FAIL_COUNT -gt 0 ]]; then
    exit 1
  fi
}

# Assertions
assert_exit_code() {
  local expected=$1 actual=$2 msg=${3:-"Exit code check"}
  if [[ "$actual" == "$expected" ]]; then
    log_pass "$msg (exit $actual)"
  else
    log_fail "$msg: expected $expected, got $actual"
  fi
}

assert_file_exists() {
  local path=$1
  if [[ -f "$path" ]]; then
    log_pass "File exists: $path"
  else
    log_fail "File missing: $path"
  fi
}
```

## Usage
```bash
source scripts/tests/lib/log.sh

log_info "Starting test..."
log_pass "Download successful"
log_fail "SHA mismatch"
test_summary
```

Test generateFilename() function that creates output filenames from platform, datetime, and index.

## Function Behavior
```javascript
// Platform + datetime: icloud_20240115_143245.jpg
// Platform + index: icloud_001.jpg  
// Platform only: icloud_<timestamp>.jpg
```

## Test Cases
1. With valid datetime and no index
2. With index and no datetime  
3. With both datetime and index
4. With neither (falls back to current timestamp)
5. Different platforms (icloud, dropbox, gphotos, gdrive)
6. Different extensions (jpg, png, webp)
7. Edge case: datetime with timezone
8. Edge case: very large index

## File
scripts/tests/filename-generation.test.mjs

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-m4d","title":"Task: Unit tests for bash functions (detect_platform, normalize_url)","description":"## What
Create unit tests for pure bash functions in giil script.

## Approach
Use bats-core (Bash Automated Testing System) for bash function testing.

## Functions to Test

### detect_platform (bash version)
```bash
@test 'detect_platform returns icloud for share.icloud.com' {
  result=$(detect_platform 'https://share.icloud.com/photos/abc123')
  [ "$result" = 'icloud' ]
}

@test 'detect_platform returns dropbox for dropbox.com/s/' {
  result=$(detect_platform 'https://www.dropbox.com/s/abc123/photo.jpg')
  [ "$result" = 'dropbox' ]
}
```

### normalize_url
```bash
@test 'normalize_url converts icloud.com/photos# to share.icloud.com' {
  result=$(normalize_url 'https://www.icloud.com/photos/#abc123')
  [ "$result" = 'https://share.icloud.com/photos/abc123' ]
}
```

### normalize_dropbox_url
```bash
@test 'normalize_dropbox_url adds raw=1' {
  result=$(normalize_dropbox_url 'https://www.dropbox.com/s/abc/photo.jpg?dl=0')
  [[ "$result" == *'raw=1'* ]]
}
```

## Setup
1. Install bats-core: `npm install -g bats`
2. Create scripts/tests/bash/giil.bats
3. Source giil functions for testing

## File
scripts/tests/bash/giil.bats

## Notes
- Bats is widely used for bash testing
- Tests run in isolated subshells
- Supports setup/teardown hooks

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-qoh","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive Test Coverage for giil v3.0","description":"## Overview

giil currently has minimal test coverage:
- 2 unit test files (platform-detection, content-validation)
- 1 integration test (iCloud only, no Dropbox/Google Photos/Google Drive)
- No error scenario tests
- No output mode tests (--base64, --preserve, --convert)
- No verbose logging in test infrastructure

## Goal

Achieve comprehensive test coverage using REAL tests (no mocks/fakes) with detailed logging for debugging. All tests should:
1. Test actual functionality against real or realistic data
2. Provide verbose, structured logging for CI debugging
3. Cover all code paths and edge cases
4. Support both local development and CI execution

## Major Gaps to Fill

### Unit Tests (Pure Functions)
- URL normalization (normalize_url, normalize_dropbox_url)
- EXIF datetime extraction (getExifDateTime)
- Filename generation (generateFilename)
- JSON output formatting (formatJsonSuccess, formatJsonError)
- Google Photos URL extraction (extractGooglePhotosBaseUrl)
- Exit code error classification
- processAndSaveImage logic branches

### Integration Tests (E2E)
- Multi-platform: iCloud, Dropbox, Google Photos, Google Drive
- Error scenarios: timeout, auth required, not found, unsupported type
- Output modes: --json, --base64, --preserve, --convert
- Album mode: --all for different platforms
- CLI flags: --verbose, --trace, --print-url, --debug-dir

### Bash Layer Tests
- CLI argument parsing
- Platform detection (bash version)
- Dependency detection
- Version consistency

### Infrastructure
- Verbose structured logging (JSON format for CI parsing)
- Test fixtures with real image files
- Replay tests for deterministic results

## Success Criteria
- 90%+ code coverage on testable pure functions
- All 4 platforms have at least 1 live integration test
- All exit codes have corresponding error scenario tests
- All CLI flags have at least 1 test
- Detailed logging makes CI failures debuggable

```javascript
  'NETWORK_ERROR': ExitCodes.NETWORK_ERROR,
  'AUTH_REQUIRED': ExitCodes.AUTH_REQUIRED,
  'NOT_FOUND': ExitCodes.NOT_FOUND,
  ...
};
```

## Test Cases
1. All error codes map to correct exit codes
2. Error codes match ExitCodes values
3. All documented exit codes (0-20) have mappings
4. Edge case: unknown error code handling

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-tfk","title":"FEATURE: Unit Test Expansion - Pure JavaScript Functions","description":"## Overview

Expand unit test coverage for all pure JavaScript functions embedded in giil. These tests use Node.js native test runner and extract functions via extract-functions.mjs.

## Current Coverage
- ✅ detectPlatform() - platform-detection.test.mjs
- ✅ validateContentType() - content-validation.test.mjs  
- ✅ validateMagicBytes() - content-validation.test.mjs
- ✅ validateImageContent() - content-validation.test.mjs

## Missing Coverage (HIGH PRIORITY)
1. **formatJsonSuccess/formatJsonError** - JSON output schema v3
2. **extractGooglePhotosBaseUrl** - Google Photos =s0 URL extraction
3. **getExifDateTime** - EXIF metadata parsing
4. **generateFilename** - Filename from datetime/index

## Missing Coverage (MEDIUM PRIORITY)
5. **downloadGooglePhotosFullRes** - Full-res download logic
6. **parseGoogleDriveId** - File ID extraction from Drive URLs
7. **getGoogleDriveDownloadUrl/getGoogleDrivePreviewUrl** - URL builders
8. **ErrorMap** - Error code to exit code mapping

## Testing Approach
- Extract functions via extract-functions.mjs (already exists)
- Use real data where possible (actual EXIF buffers, real URLs)
- Each function gets its own describe() block
- Test edge cases: null, undefined, empty, malformed inputs

## Files to Create/Modify
- scripts/tests/json-formatting.test.mjs (NEW)
- scripts/tests/google-photos.test.mjs (NEW)
- scripts/tests/exif-datetime.test.mjs (NEW)
- scripts/tests/filename-generation.test.mjs (NEW)
- scripts/tests/error-mapping.test.mjs (NEW)
- scripts/tests/extract-functions.mjs (MODIFY - add new extractors)

## Dependencies
- Requires extract-functions.mjs updates to export new functions
- Some tests need sample image buffers (JPEG with EXIF, HEIC, etc.)

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-v03","title":"Task: E2E tests for error scenarios (timeout, auth, not found)","description":"## What
Create E2E tests that verify correct exit codes for error scenarios.

## Test Script
scripts/tests/e2e/error-scenarios.test.sh

## Error Scenarios to Test

### EXIT 10: Network Error
```bash
# Use known unreachable host or set very short timeout
giil 'https://share.icloud.com/photos/xxx' --timeout 1
assert_exit_code 10
```

### EXIT 11: Auth Required
```bash
# Use a non-public iCloud link (if available)
# Or use a restricted Google Drive file
giil 'https://drive.google.com/file/d/RESTRICTED_FILE/view'
assert_exit_code 11
```

### EXIT 12: Not Found
```bash
# Use an expired/deleted iCloud link
giil 'https://share.icloud.com/photos/NONEXISTENT12345'
assert_exit_code 12
```

### EXIT 13: Unsupported Type
```bash
# Use a video link or Google Doc
giil 'https://docs.google.com/document/d/xxx/view'
assert_exit_code 13
```

## JSON Error Response Verification
Also verify JSON error structure:
```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "schema_version": "1",
  "platform": "...",
  "error": {
    "code": "AUTH_REQUIRED",
    "message": "...",
    "remediation": "..."
  }
}
```

## Notes
- Some error scenarios need stable 'bad' URLs
- May need to create dedicated test resources
- Exit codes must match documented values exactly

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-yyd","title":"Task: E2E test for Google Photos platform","description":"## What
Create E2E integration test for Google Photos downloads with =s0 full-resolution modifier.

## Test Script
scripts/tests/e2e/google-photos.test.sh

## Prerequisites
- Need a stable Google Photos test URL (public album)
- Store expected SHA256 in fixtures

## Test Steps
1. Download from photos.app.goo.gl short URL
2. Verify full resolution obtained (=s0 modifier used)
3. Verify output file exists and is valid image
4. Verify JSON output schema (platform: 'gphotos')
5. Log resolution to confirm full-res

## Verbose Logging
```
[TIME] [INFO] Platform: gphotos
[TIME] [INFO] URL: https://photos.app.goo.gl/...
[TIME] [INFO] Browser launched
[TIME] [INFO] CDN URL extracted: lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/...
[TIME] [INFO] Applied =s0 modifier for full resolution
[TIME] [INFO] Downloaded: 4032x3024 (12.1 MB)
[TIME] [PASS] Resolution matches expected full-res
[TIME] [PASS] SHA256 verified
```

## Also Test
- photos.google.com/share/ long URL format
- Verify =s0 modifier is applied (check verbose output)

## Notes
- Google Photos URLs tend to be less stable than iCloud
- May need periodic URL refresh if test starts failing

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-ztt","title":"Task: Unit tests for JSON formatting (formatJsonSuccess, formatJsonError)","description":"## What
Create unit tests for JSON output formatting functions that produce v3 schema.

## Functions to Test

### formatJsonSuccess(data)
Wraps data with schema_version, ok=true, platform fields.

### formatJsonError(code, message, remediation)
Creates error response with ok=false, error object.

## Test Cases

### formatJsonSuccess
```javascript
it('wraps data with schema_version and ok fields', () => {
  const result = formatJsonSuccess({ path: '/test.jpg', method: 'network' });
  assert.strictEqual(result.ok, true);
  assert.strictEqual(result.schema_version, '1');
  assert.ok(result.path);
});

it('preserves all input fields', () => { ... });
it('handles empty object input', () => { ... });
```

### formatJsonError
```javascript
it('creates error object with code and message', () => {
  const result = formatJsonError('AUTH_REQUIRED', 'Login needed', 'Enable sharing');
  assert.strictEqual(result.ok, false);
  assert.strictEqual(result.error.code, 'AUTH_REQUIRED');
  assert.strictEqual(result.error.message, 'Login needed');
  assert.strictEqual(result.error.remediation, 'Enable sharing');
});

it('handles null remediation', () => { ... });
```

## File
scripts/tests/json-formatting.test.mjs

## Requires
- Update extract-functions.mjs to export formatJsonSuccess, formatJsonError
